title: Innodata Bets on Agentic AI: Is it the Next Revenue Wave?
text: Innodata Agentic AI Frontier Models and Customer Wins. Customer Wins: New 2026 engagement with major Big Tech customer worth about $51M in revenue, representing contracted work. Deepened ties with largest client and secured $10 million in new second-half engagements with another top tech firm—up sharply from just $200,000 a year earlier. January 2026: INOD selected by Palantir to support advanced AI deployments involving complex multimodal data—an example of agent-like systems operating in high-stakes environments where precision and reliability are critical. Agentic AI Focus: Innodata built specialized judge frameworks—task-success, diagnostic and profiling judges—that help big-tech model builders evaluate and fine-tune autonomous agents operating in real-world workflows. These capabilities increasingly embedded in reinforcement learning pipelines, making them recurring rather than one-off services. Agentic systems demand continuous iteration, monitoring and retraining, expanding lifetime value of customer engagements. Frontier Model Developer Work: For $51 million contract, INOD provides pretraining, mid-training, and post-training data. Agentic AI work often builds on existing relationships with hyperscalers and frontier model developers, allowing INOD to layer higher-value services on core data-engineering base.
